summary: Set 022 Session 2 (commits dcc8636 + this close-out commit): shipped v0.13.12 of the Dabbler AI Orchestration extension with reader-side support for the state-first lifecycle protocol Session 1 delivered. Dropped activity-log unique-sessionNumber and currentSession-1 as count sources; added countDistinctCloseoutSessions() as the Full-tier events-ledger fallback. Surfaced LiveSession.completedSessions through the type system. progressText gained ` Done` and ` · session N in flight` annotations driven by a new isCurrentSessionInFlight() predicate that implements the spec invariant (currentSession not in completedSessions[]) and guards against legacy snapshots. File watcher pattern extended to include session-events.jsonl + CANCELLED.md so boundary writes from start_session / close_session and cancel/restore commands trigger immediate refresh. 5 new fileSystem regression tests + 13 new SessionSetsProvider tests + 1 round-1-fix regression test added. Cross-provider verification (gpt-5-4): round 1 ISSUES_FOUND with 1 real Major (state-file vs activity-log totalSessions precedence) + 1 context-gap false positive — both addressed in round 2; round 2 VERIFIED. v0.13.12.vsix packaged successfully. Live readSessionSets against set 022 renders `1/3 · session 2 in flight` as the spec demanded.

reason.specifics: Session 3 is documentation + cross-consumer verification work — the canonical workflow doc, schema doc, and close-out doc all need their voice matched to existing prose. Same orchestrator that wrote Sessions 1 and 2 has the freshest context on the invariant and the spec's framing; switching engines mid-set would force re-learning the protocol's vocabulary.
